target.name: Theseus
target.founder: Carl Schoeller, Ian Laffey, Sacha Lévy

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Theseus is a seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector. The peer universe comprises 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The broader comp set includes 2 public companies and 8 private peers spanning the sector.

The peer-median method was the applicable triangulation approach given available data. The median valuation across the 4 selected peers is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M. This method directly reflects market valuations for companies at Theseus's stage and sector, providing a grounded anchor for valuation.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $191M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $225M. This range reflects the observed spread in the peer set, particularly the distance between Tenna Systems at seed stage ($60M) and the Series A comparables. Key uncertainties include the specificity of Theseus's technology relative to peers, customer traction relative to stage norms, and the extent to which seed-stage Defense & Security companies command stage-appropriate discounts to Series A valuations. A single-method analysis limits confidence; additional methods (cost-of-capital, revenue multiple analysis) would strengthen conviction in this range.
